message:
🌱 prometheus: Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody (#1920)

* 🌱 Add prometheus: Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody mission

* 🌱 Add prometheus: Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "prometheus-3441-prometheus-docker-container-shouldn-t-run-as-user-nobody",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "prometheus: Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody",
+    "description": "Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody. Requested by 53+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current prometheus deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your prometheus version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus\nhelm list -n prometheus 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working prometheus installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review prometheus configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus\nkubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus\n```\nWith the update to 2.0, the Docker container was modified so that Prometheus runs as the user \"nobody\" (UID/GID 99/99) (#2859). While it's good that Prometheus is no longer running as root, it's not the best idea either to run as \"nobody\"."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody",
+        "description": "Unfortunately the solution outlined by [ohritz](https://github.com/ohritz) [on Jul 2, 2023](https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/3441#issuecomment-1616114258):\n\nWon't work here – that is OpenShift specific, whereas the Prometheus image needs to work under Docker, standard Kubernetes and\n```yaml\n$ docker run --user 1234:5678 --rm -ti busybox\n/ $ id\nuid=1234 gid=5678\n/ $\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus\nkubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Unfortunately the solution outlined by [ohritz](https://github.com/ohritz) [on Jul 2, 2023](https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/3441#issuecomment-1616114258):\n\nWon't work here – that is OpenShift specific, whereas the Prometheus image needs to work under Docker, standard Kubernetes and anything else that can run OCI containers.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "$ docker run --user 1234:5678 --rm -ti busybox\n/ $ id\nuid=1234 gid=5678\n/ $",
+        "$ docker run -it --rm --entrypoint sh prom/prometheus:v2.0.0\n\n/prometheus $ id\nuid=65534(nobody) gid=65534(nogroup)"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "prometheus",
+      "graduated",
+      "observability",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "prometheus"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/3441",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus"
+    },
+    "reactions": 53,
+    "comments": 25,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-02T06:32:53.653Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "prometheus-3441-prometheus-docker-container-shouldn-t-run-as-user-nobody",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "prometheus: Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody",
+    "description": "Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody. Requested by 53+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current prometheus deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your prometheus version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus\nhelm list -n prometheus 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working prometheus installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review prometheus configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant prometheus configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus\nkubectl get configmap -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=prometheus\n```\nWith the update to 2.0, the Docker container was modified so that Prometheus runs as the user \"nobody\" (UID/GID 99/99) (#2859). While it's good that Prometheus is no longer running as root, it's not the best idea either to run as \"nobody\"."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody",
+        "description": "I re-wrote the `Dockerfile` to be more in line with common `Dockerfile`s.\n\nThe only significant difference is the user running promgen: I removed the promgen user, that means that the web app and the worker are now run as root. This simplifies the permissions required for the configuration files.\n```yaml\n$ docker run --user 1234:5678 --rm -ti busybox\r\n/ $ id\r\nuid=1234 gid=5678\r\n/ $\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n prometheus -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus\nkubectl get events -n prometheus --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Prometheus docker container shouldn't run as user nobody\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "I re-wrote the `Dockerfile` to be more in line with common `Dockerfile`s.\n\nThe only significant difference is the user running promgen: I removed the promgen user, that means that the web app and the worker are now run as root. This simplifies the permissions required for the configuration files. Running applications in docker as root is a common strategy.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "$ docker run --user 1234:5678 --rm -ti busybox\r\n/ $ id\r\nuid=1234 gid=5678\r\n/ $",
+        "$ docker run -it --rm --entrypoint sh prom/prometheus:v2.0.0\r\n\r\n/prometheus $ id\r\nuid=65534(nobody) gid=65534(nogroup)",
+        "Can see the user in the container with"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "prometheus",
+      "graduated",
+      "observability",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "prometheus"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/3441",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/line/promgen/pull/56"
+    },
+    "reactions": 53,
+    "comments": 25,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
